examples.title: Algorithm: Construct RelativeLayout
examples.description: Positioning views relative to parent and sibling elements
examples.code:
ALGORITHM BuildRelativeLayout
BEGIN
 1. Create RelativeLayout container
 2. Set layout dimensions (match_parent/wrap_content)
 3. Add child views with rules:
 a. ALIGN_PARENT_TOP/BOTTOM/START/END
 b. BELOW/ABOVE, TO_START_OF/TO_END_OF
 4. Handle view dependencies sequentially
END
examples.explanation: Step 3a: Positions view relative to parent. Step 3b: Creates relationships between views. Step 4: Ensures referenced views exist before use.

examples.title: Algorithm: Create TableLayout
examples.description: Building a table structure with dynamic rows/columns
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ConstructTable
BEGIN
 1. Initialize TableLayout
 2. Set stretchColumns/shrinkColumns properties
 3. FOR each row:
 a. Create TableRow
 b. Add views as cells
 c. Set cell layout_weight if needed
 d. Add row to TableLayout
END
examples.explanation: Step 2: Controls column resizing. Step 3b: Each view becomes a table cell. Step 3c: Manages proportional column sizing.

examples.title: Java: RelativeLayout Implementation
examples.description: Programmatic creation of login form layout
examples.explanation: Username field centered at top. Password field positioned below username with same horizontal alignment.

examples.title: Java: TableLayout Implementation
examples.explanation: First row contains headers. Subsequent rows show multiplication results. setStretchAllColumns ensures equal width distribution.
